| bug-003-builder-output-contract-mismatch    | bug           | in-progress | P0       | fix/builder-output-contract-mismatch    | Orchestrator's `translateOutcomes` parser expects `taskOutcomes: { id: status }` map; canonical `BuilderOutput` schema (+ all 3 builder agent prompts) emits `tasksCompleted: BuilderTaskResult[]` array. Result: agent succeeds but is marked failed with "agent produced no parseable outcome JSON". Surfaced cheaply by bug-002's TASK_RETRY_CAP=1 ($1.70 instead of $6) on kanban-webapp 2026-04-25.                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| bug-004-agent-output-format-schema          | bug           | in-progress | P0       | fix/agent-output-format-schema          | Orchestrator's `buildAgentOptions` never declares `outputFormat` so the SDK runs in plain-text mode; fallback regex `/\{[\s\S]*\}\s*$/` for trailing JSON breaks on markdown fences / trailing prose. Same surface error as bug-003 ("agent produced no parseable outcome JSON") but earlier guard. Fix: use SDK's `outputFormat: { type: 'json_schema', schema: BuilderOutputJsonSchema }` for builder agents. Surfaced at $1.33 on kanban-webapp 2026-04-26.                                                                                                                                           |
| bug-005-windows-quoting-and-default-branch  | bug           | in-progress | P0       | fix/windows-quoting-and-default-branch  | TWO bugs surfacing together on Windows+master-default projects: (a) `commitWorktreeChanges` uses `git commit -m '<msg>'` which on cmd.exe parses single quotes as literals → task IDs become pathspecs → every auto-commit fails; (b) `runCloseFeature` hardcodes `main` in 4 git ops → fails on `master`-default projects → falls into phantom merge-conflict retries → emergency-abort. Fix: tempfile-based `git commit -F <path>` + `detectDefaultBranch()` helper. Surfaced at $8.64 on kanban-webapp 2026-04-26.                                                                                    |
| bug-006-greedy-json-extractor               | bug           | in-progress | P0       | fix/greedy-json-extractor               | `extractStructuredOutput`'s greedy regex `/\{[\s\S]*\}\s*$/` matches first `{` to last `}` — when agent prose contains `{` chars (destructuring examples, type defs), it swallows everything between them and the trailing status JSON → JSON.parse fails. Agent succeeded fully (50 tests, 94% coverage, MERGED to master autonomously); orchestrator just couldn't see. Fix: backward-scan from each `{` looking for valid trailing JSON. Surfaced at $1.35 on kanban-webapp 2026-04-26.                                                                                                               |
| bug-007-robust-output-extraction            | bug           | in-progress | P0       | fix/robust-output-extraction            | 4-strategy parser stack to stop returning to this layer: (1) SDK structured_output, (2) `<<<TASK_OUTCOME>>>` sentinel-delimited block, (3) balanced-brace forward scan respecting JSON strings, (4) diagnostic failure. Replaces bug-006's "ends-with-`}`" assumption that breaks on backtick-wrapped / prose-trailing JSON. Adds sentinel addendum to `buildAgentPrompt`. Surfaced at $2.52 on kanban-webapp-01 2026-04-26 (run 8).                                                                                                                                                                     |
| bug-008-close-feature-dirty-root            | bug           | in-progress | P0       | fix/close-feature-dirty-root            | close-feature's `git merge --no-ff` aborts BEFORE touching HEAD when project root has uncommitted/untracked files overlapping with feature-branch paths (git protects untracked from overwrite). Misclassified as merge-conflict → 3× resolve-conflict-handoff dispatches → emergency-abort → branch deleted → all agent commits orphaned. Killed every Mode B run since bug-007 cleared parser. Diagnostic patch already applied; Phase 1 fix: pre-flight auto-commit dirty project root before merge. Surfaced at $6.43 on kanban-webapp-02 2026-04-26 (run 10).                                       |
| bug-009-checkout-feature-snapshot           | bug           | in-progress | P0       | fix/checkout-feature-snapshot           | bug-008's snapshot fired at WRONG TIME (close-feature, after agent had recreated kit) → AA (add/add) merge conflicts on `packages/ui-kit/{package.json, src/index.ts, src/lib/cn.ts}` that resolve-conflict-handoff couldn't unstick. Move snapshot to checkout-feature (BEFORE worktree creation) so the worktree branches from kit-inclusive state, agent consumes existing kit per its prompt, no recreation, clean merge. Surfaced at $5.53 on kanban-webapp-03 2026-04-26 (run 11). **First autonomous merge `087321c` achieved on kanban-webapp-04 2026-04-26 (run 12) — MVP exit threshold met.** |
| bug-010-graceful-skip-unknown-agent         | bug           | in-progress | P0       | fix/graceful-skip-unknown-agent         | Orchestrator throws unhandled exception when `agent_sequence[]` references an unshipped agent (e.g., PM emits `security` per its design, factory hasn't shipped `.claude/agents/security.md` yet). One feature crashes ENTIRE Mode B run including N-1 sibling features. Fix: try/catch in `runLlmAgent` around `readModelConfig`; on unknown agent return `taskStatus: completed` + `skippedReason` + warning, advance agent_sequence. Committed `b53e8ed` 2026-04-26.                                                                                                                                  |

| bug-013-feature-context-gitignore           | bug           | in-progress | P0       | fix/feature-context-gitignore           | `.feature-context.json` is per-worktree runtime state (agent_history, last_writing_agent, status) but not gitignored — every parallel feature commits a different version → AA add/add merge conflict on master after first feature lands. | bug-012-lockfile-aware-conflict-resolution  | bug           | draft       | P0       | fix/lockfile-aware-conflict-resolution  | resolve-conflict-handoff dispatches LLM agent to text-merge `pnpm-lock.yaml` / `package-lock.json` / `yarn.lock` — structurally non-mergeable; agent's output rarely matches merged manifest → 3× retry cap exhausts → emergency-abort. Fix: orchestrator-side `tryAutoResolveLockfileConflicts` preprocessor in `runCloseFeature` (lockfile-only conflicts: `git checkout --theirs` + `pnpm install --lockfile-only` + `git add` + commit) + builder/reviewer prompt addendum for mixed conflicts + export to all 13 projects. Surfaced at $20.29 on kanban-webapp-05 2026-04-26 (feat-settings-data).  |
